comments.author: @真なる大悪魔
comments.text: This was a nice documentary. I definitely connected based on my own times growing up yet I could not help but to envision hydroponic vertical farms, bicycle and bicycle routes, solar power modern Yurts/ Ger with biodegradeable waterless toilets, solar powered herbal centre with an herbalist, chiropactor, masseuse and mid-wives; a recreational centre which include their traditional sports along with modern sports of choice and a fitness center all solar powered centre and natural resistance machines with an olympic swimming pool. Everything all natural as the final upgrade along with keep traditions in place. And in each and every solar powered Yurt/Ger I would have cast iron stoves, standing shower alone with a separate stand alone stone tub, wood tub or protected metal tub. Lastly there is a way to populate the desert with green but I would first want to know what is beneath the sand first because i would honestly construct a sand castle fitting the mongol architecture and incorporating cactus which grow edible fruit along with the cactus itself being edible but seriously built a sand castle of just stairs to step up, around, down leading out to various directions into the land. I love this documentary of Mongolia because it got my brain juices flowing early in the morning.

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you for taking the time to leave such a profound and sincere comment under the video. We are delighted that the video touched your emotions. This motivates us to explore even more fascinating lands. Please subscribe to the channel so you don't miss out on the world's most beautiful landscapes.
